message:
fix(install): prevent unbound variable error in EXIT trap

When main() returns, the local variable temp_dir goes out of scope,
but the EXIT trap fires at global scope where temp_dir is unbound.
This causes an error when running with set -u (nounset option).

Using ${temp_dir:-} provides an empty default when the variable is
unset, preventing the unbound variable error.

Fixes #6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -245,7 +245,7 @@ main() {
     # Create temp directory
     local temp_dir
     temp_dir=$(mktemp -d)
-    trap 'rm -rf "$temp_dir"' EXIT
+    trap 'rm -rf "${temp_dir:-}"' EXIT
 
     # Build download URLs
     # Adjust version for URL (strip 'v' prefix if present)
